text:
| [#210](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/210) | **Retrospective instrumentation** | `tenex-edge explain <handle>` + an `llm_calls` ledger — your "what was fed to the LLM?" question |

**#210** is the one that directly answers your motivating use case: a `tenex-edge explain <handle>` verb over artifact handles (`event:`, `hook:`, `llm:`, `txn:`, `session:@ts`), backed by a persisted `txn_receipts` table and an `llm_calls` ledger that captures the exact transcript slice + system prompt + model + raw response behind every distilled "current activity" — plus `replay` via trace-matching and a redaction policy for the user content in prompts.
